channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 19-year-old woman fatally attacked by dog in Essex
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Essex where a 19-year-old woman lost her life following a dog attack. The authorities have confirmed that emergency services were called to the scene after the attack, bu...

item.title: Liberals Engage With Several MPs About Potential Floor Crossings
item.description: Sources indicate that the Liberal Party is actively engaging with as many as eight opposition MPs regarding the possibility of crossing the floor. The discussions involve both Conservative and New Dem...

item.title: Authorities seize P1.1 billion in smuggled cigarettes in Lapu-Lapu City
item.description: Authorities in Lapu-Lapu City, Cebu, conducted a successful raid on a warehouse in Barangay Basak where smuggled cigarettes valued at P1.1 billion were discovered. The operation led to the seizure of ...

item.title: Courtney Barnett finds creativity again with new album after battling writer’s block
item.description: Australian indie rock musician Courtney Barnett has released her new album, Creature of Habit, marking the end of a lengthy struggle with writer’s block. The 38-year-old artist, now based in Los Angel...

item.title: Historic US-Iran Negotiations Begin in Islamabad with Pakistan as Mediator
item.description: For the first time since 1979, senior US and Iranian officials have met face-to-face in Islamabad, Pakistan, in talks aimed at securing peace in the Middle East. The American delegation is led by Vice...

item.title: Anticyclonic wave near Oman expected to cause warmer and drier weather in Karachi and Sindh
item.description: Karachi is expected to face hotter and drier conditions over the coming days as an anticyclonic wave situated near the Oman coast influences the regional weather pattern. According to meteorological a...

item.title: Rigid TSC recruitment rules leave thousands of qualified teachers jobless
item.description: In Kenya, hundreds of graduate teachers are struggling to find employment because of strict Teachers Service Commission (TSC) regulations. Graduates like Getray Munyelele and Ann Thuo, despite holding...

item.title: Sky News Money Newsletter Page Inaccessible
item.description: The provided link to the Sky News Money Newsletter cannot be accessed, as the server displays an 'Access Denied' message. This message indicates that the webpage is restricted or unavailable due to pe...

item.title: Early Breakfast and Longer Overnight Fasting Associated with Lower BMI
item.description: A recent study conducted by the Barcelona Institute for Global Health (ISGlobal) suggests that meal timing may be as important as food choices in maintaining a healthy weight. The research, involving ...

item.title: Tanzania and Shelter Afrique join hands to boost affordable housing financing across Africa
item.description: At the Second African Urban Forum held in Nairobi from April 8 to 10, 2026, Tanzania’s Minister for Lands, Housing and Human Settlements Development, Dr Leonard Akwilapo, met with Shelter Afrique Chie...

item.title: Former Nasarawa Deputy Governor John Michael-Abdul emerges as ADC State Chairman
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Nasarawa State don elect former Deputy Governor, John Michael-Abdul, as their new state chairman. The congress take place for Lafia on Saturday, with delegates...

item.title: Rory McIlroy sets Masters record with six-shot lead after second round 65
item.description: Rory McIlroy produced a brilliant second round at the 2026 US Masters in Augusta, Georgia, firing a seven-under 65 to take a commanding six-shot lead at the halfway mark. This performance set a new Ma...

item.title: Netherlands Approves Tesla's Supervised Self-Driving Feature for Drivers
item.description: The Netherlands has become the first European country to permit Tesla vehicles to use their Full Self-Driving (FSD) Supervised feature on public roads. While drivers must remain in the vehicle and act...

item.title: IEBC achieves over 875,000 new voter registrations halfway through nationwide listing campaign
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has recorded a total of 875,501 new voter registrations within the first two weeks of its ongoing nationwide mass listing exercise. The campa...

item.title: U.S. Military Operations Reported in the Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions with Iran
item.description: On April 11, 2026, President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social that U.S. forces had begun clearing the Strait of Hormuz and claimed that all of Iran’s minelaying vessels had been sunk. The announcem...

item.title: Liverpool face Fulham amid fan protests and seek to regain form under Arne Slot
item.description: Liverpool hosted Fulham at Anfield in a crucial Premier League clash as they looked to halt a poor run of results under coach Arne Slot. The game opened with subdued energy from the home side, as earl...

item.title: Manipur’s football culture driving India’s women’s success at FIFA 2026 Series
item.description: Manipur, a small state found in India’s far northeast, continues to stand out as a powerhouse in women’s football. Though it has a modest population, the area has produced most of the players represen...

item.title: I Am Maximus makes history with second Grand National victory for Willie Mullins
item.description: I Am Maximus claimed a remarkable second Grand National triumph at Aintree, becoming the first horse since Red Rum in 1977 to regain the title. Trained by Irishman Willie Mullins, the 2024 winner and ...

item.title: Cannes 2026 Highlights Strong International Presence Amid Reduced Hollywood Studio Participation
item.description: The 2026 Cannes Film Festival lineup was revealed, emphasizing international auteurs such as Pedro Almodóvar, Hirokazu Kore-eda, and Asghar Farhadi. While there is some representation from Hollywood a...

item.title: South Africans push for legalised medically assisted dying after years of suffering
item.description: For more than ten years, Dieter Harck, 76, has been battling for the right to decide how he dies, following his diagnosis with motor neuron disease, a debilitating and progressive condition. Harck exp...
